128208.explanation: Important issues should certainly be submitted to the residents.

128209.explanation: Agree when it concerns a park or other public green spaces. Disagree when it comes to a sports hall used by many users.

128211.explanation: Just like shops recently started setting their own opening hours, this should also apply to cafes and restaurants, as long as this extension does not lead to more nuisance. Otherwise, the hours will be restricted again.

128212.explanation: The municipality receives no funds from the government for this. ABZ stands for no money, no tasks, so when the government provides us with the resources, we can pass them on to such initiatives.

128214.explanation: In March 2027 the agreement for shelter in Hotel Ara expires. At that time, no new location will be made available.

128215.explanation: But in case of nuisance because people do not comply with the burning guidelines, action may be taken.

128218.explanation: Bulky waste can be delivered free of charge at the recycling center and can be collected from home for only €20.

128219.explanation: The standards regarding time and amount of noise are adequately regulated in Zwijndrecht.

128221.explanation: The support the broadcaster currently receives is sufficient.

128226.explanation: The focus of the enforcers could indeed shift more towards safety instead of, for example, dog waste. More enforcers are not currently planned, as it was recently expanded.

128227.explanation: In places where this is necessary, it should definitely be deployed. This also applies to the waste containers, where many side placements occur. A decision called 'Lik op stuk' has been made to address this.

128229.explanation: In a place like Zwijndrecht this is not yet necessary. If this changes in the future, we will assess it at that time.

128235.explanation: Zwijndrecht is not a student city, so in our view there is no need for this. If that changes, we can assess it at that moment.

128236.explanation: If someone is physically able to do so, that should certainly be an option. It has the additional advantage that it is easy to grow from 'work' to different work.

128238.explanation: This is currently set at 130 percent and has already been increased. We do not see any point in further increasing it. Moreover, again, no money, no tasks applies here.

128241.explanation: In the residential neighborhoods of Zwijndrecht the speed is already set to 30 km/h. Some main roads are moving towards this speed. However, we will not introduce this as the 'rule' for all of Zwijndrecht.

128243.explanation: Heavy traffic destined for industrial areas can reach these companies outside the built-up area. Thus, by introducing an environmental zone, you oblige these vehicles to use this route and not drive through the residential area.

128244.explanation: This is not a task for the municipal government. We will therefore not deal with it.

128246.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality. Therefore, we will not concern ourselves with it.

128247.explanation: If this would be possible, it must certainly be among the options. So also look at the amount of money you intended to raise and what you actually raised. If this is more than budgeted, that part should be returned to the residents.

128249.explanation: We will not expand to, for example, the outer areas. In the surrounding municipalities there is still plenty of space, so why should we do so and add more ugly boxes to our green environment?

128250.explanation: We recently decided to extend shopping hours to offer more options. We leave it to the entrepreneur to decide whether to make use of it, and to the resident to decide whether to take advantage of Sunday shopping.

128251.explanation: At this moment, we are forced to do so by for example the national government and the province. If it were up to ABZ, this would no longer be the case and they would be put on the list just like everyone else. When their turn comes, they will be assigned a home.

128255.explanation: We have tried several times to restart this in our water-rich environment, but the schools in Zwijndrecht do not want this. Without them, it can be introduced. We do allocate funds so that no child leaves primary school without a swimming diploma.

128257.explanation: Zwijndrecht currently has a percentage of social rental housing well above the national average. This is in contrast to our neighboring municipality Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht. In our plans we keep the number stable but lower the percentage.

128263.explanation: That is already a done deal. ABZ did request this, but the majority decided otherwise. So raising this now for our part of the Noordoevers is outdated. For the part of Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht, given their 20% social rental housing, this should certainly be considered.

128264.explanation: At the behest of the SGP-CU, research is being conducted here to see what possibilities exist.

128265.explanation: Heavy traffic to industrial sites can reach these outside the built-up area. This traffic does not belong on this route due to pollution and safety for our school-going youth.

128266.explanation: The success of Walburg, with hardly any vacancies, is partly due to the abolition of paid parking. We will not reintroduce it.

128208.explanation: We support resident involvement, but not through referenda. Important issues are often too complex for a yes/no choice. We prefer good participation beforehand and careful decision-making by the municipal council. The council remains ultimately responsible.

128209.explanation: We believe in the power of the community. Residents who want to take over management of a sports hall or park deserve room and trust. The municipality supports where needed but encourages responsibility and involvement. Together, this strengthens livability.

128211.explanation: Opening hours affect not only entrepreneurs but also peace and livability. We want to prevent pressure to stay open late or on Sundays. That is why we choose clear frameworks from the municipality.

128212.explanation: Generating energy together enhances community spirit and sustainability. Financial support helps residents make these initiatives feasible. Together, we contribute to a green future for Zwijndrecht and Heerjansdam.

128214.explanation: Everyone deserves a safe place. Closing asylum shelters solves nothing and harms vulnerable people. We choose humanity and cooperation to ensure good shelter that contributes to peace and order in our municipality.

128215.explanation: A ban is unenforceable. To limit nuisance, the municipality can start an information campaign.

128218.explanation: Free bulky waste collection prevents littering and illegal dumping. It makes cleaning accessible to everyone and contributes to a clean, safe living environment. Together, this strengthens livability in our municipality.

128219.explanation: Stricter noise standards protect health and livability. Festivals and parties remain possible but with clear limits so neighbors do not experience unnecessary nuisance.

128221.explanation: The local broadcaster is valuable, but extra funding is not feasible within responsible financial policy. We choose cooperation and efficiency, not structural increases in municipal taxes.

128223.explanation: Music lessons are valuable, but full subsidies for all children are not compatible with responsible financial policy. We choose targeted support through funds so children from low-income families can participate.

128226.explanation: In the past four years, the availability of enforcement officers (boa's) has more than tripled.

128227.explanation: More camera surveillance infringes on privacy and is not a structural solution for safety. We choose prevention, community work, and visible enforcement, such as boa's, instead of extra cameras. This keeps safety humane and proportional.

128229.explanation: Boa's have an important role in safety and enforcement. A baton increases their resilience in high-risk situations and helps prevent escalation. This way, we ensure a safe environment for residents and enforcement officers.

128235.explanation: Student housing is important, but temporary solutions require a lot of money and space. We choose structural cooperation with the region and educational institutions. It is not a municipal task.

128236.explanation: Mandatory reciprocal obligations sound logical but do not work. We opt for guidance and customization to help people sustainably find work or another meaningful daily activity, not coercion.

128238.explanation: More and more families struggle to make ends meet. By raising the threshold, we also reach middle incomes that currently fall outside support. This prevents debts and social problems. Raising the income limit is costly but will help more families.

128241.explanation: A 30 km/h speed limit increases traffic safety and reduces noise nuisance. This makes our neighborhoods safer for children, cyclists, and pedestrians.

128243.explanation: Near the railway, rivers, and highway, the effect of an environmental zone is not measurable.

128244.explanation: Care must connect with the living environment of residents. Freedom of choice strengthens trust and quality of assistance. This keeps care humane and respectful.

128246.explanation: No one should be excluded from menstrual products due to poverty. The existing free provision through, among others, schools, food banks, and clothing banks has a positive effect. It prevents exclusion and, for example, unnecessary school absenteeism.

128247.explanation: In recent years, the property tax (ozb) increased less than inflation. Lowering it leads to new cuts that are undesirable.

128249.explanation: There is currently no room left for expansion of existing businesses or establishment of new companies. This hinders job growth.

128250.explanation: In addition to our principled position on Sunday shopping, the proposals from 2018 and 2025 are insufficiently substantiated. Support among shopkeepers has not been well researched and only a small part benefits.

128251.explanation: The housing crisis demands a fair distribution of the scarce housing opportunities. This always requires customization in the allocation of homes. But it also requires new creative solutions for new residents who have no rental home available.

128255.explanation: More than 98 percent of children leave primary school with a swimming diploma. Still, restoring school swimming would help parents (regardless of income) to enable children to earn their diploma faster. This increases safety in our water-rich municipality.

128257.explanation: We maintain a better balance in the population structure. The number of rental homes can remain the same. By mainly adding mid-priced and higher-priced homes, we stimulate moving up for families and ensure a more balanced housing market.

128263.explanation: We choose not to add social housing but to utilize the North Shores housing program for more and better flow-through for families.

128264.explanation: The municipality has relatively low vacancy. A rental obligation would severely restrict the freedom of homeowners.

128265.explanation: The route from the Groote Lindt business park via the blue bridge is designed for heavier and larger freight traffic. The only alternative is the route via Munnikensteeg. These are extra kilometers, resulting in extra emissions and shifting traffic pressure.

128266.explanation: The attractiveness of the shopping center is under pressure due to paid parking. The financial benefit for the municipality is limited.

128208.explanation: Asking residents' opinions via a referendum or other means increases involvement in society and democratic decision-making. A condition is also involvement after the referendum.

128209.explanation: It is good to try other management methods, both in terms of costs and to leave more to the community.

128211.explanation: We think it is good to do this in consultation with entrepreneurs, visitors, and the environmental service.

128212.explanation: It is good to encourage this; energy issues are increasingly difficult to solve. People who want to commit to this topic should be supported.

128214.explanation: We have a legal obligation to arrange asylum reception, and we believe it should be done properly.

128215.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves cause air pollution and are harmful to neighbors with lung complaints.

128218.explanation: The bulky waste collection service in Zwijndrecht is complicated and expensive. This can really be improved; for people without transportation and low income, it should be free, with a yearly limit.

128219.explanation: Noise is part of festivals and celebrations. A permit must be requested; this sets the noise standards.

128221.explanation: Yes, otherwise the local broadcaster would no longer exist, and it serves a good function for residents.

128223.explanation: Yes, musical education is good for children's development. It stimulates creativity, cognitive, and motor development.

128226.explanation: There are already sufficient boa's; they should mainly focus on the tasks they are appointed for.

128227.explanation: Only if really necessary and always with accompanying measures to address nuisance.

128229.explanation: Boa's are not trained for this. We also believe this belongs to the police, who should be deployed in case of escalation. However, boa's must be well equipped and supported to perform their tasks properly.

128235.explanation: All alternative forms of housing are worth trying; I believe there is certainly a need for this in Zwijndrecht.

128236.explanation: Yes, in consultation with those involved; it must be appropriate and stimulating for development. If people are not capable due to illness or disability, then not.

128238.explanation: The amount of income says nothing about poverty; this can have various causes, and the solutions are diverse.

128241.explanation: Yes, where possible certainly, but not everywhere. On main roads, this is not always practical because they are designed as 50 km/h zones.

128243.explanation: Polluting vehicles do not belong in built-up areas.

128244.explanation: Everyone has the right to appropriate care when necessary, and where possible, indicators such as religion, culture, and beliefs are included. The focus remains on providing the necessary care.

128246.explanation: Tampons and sanitary pads are expensive; not everyone can afford these products, which is undesirable. At the food bank and other places, tampons and pads are made available for girls and women who cannot afford them. We consider this necessary.

128247.explanation: Municipalities need certain revenues to carry out their work. A reduction of the property tax would be nice, but it is not an end in itself.

128249.explanation: Expansion is possible on designated business parks; there is still room for expansion in Zwijndrecht. It would be good to cluster this better so that perhaps space can be freed up for housing construction.

128250.explanation: We are not in favor of Sunday shopping. Often, only the large supermarkets are open. Small entrepreneurs cannot staff this, and additionally, opening one more day is bad for the environment. Being open six days a week is sufficient to do shopping.

128251.explanation: The municipality is legally required to house refugees. This is currently done according to certain rules. We do not support abandoning these rules.

128255.explanation: We believe it is important that all children learn to swim, and swimming lessons are very expensive and unaffordable for many parents. School swimming is a good solution here.

128257.explanation: Better than 35% social rental housing is 40%. There are extremely long waiting lists; for large groups, owning a home is not an option, and the waiting time lasts years.

128263.explanation: Currently, more expensive homes are planned; social housing was demolished for this. We support building more social housing, including on the Noordoevers.

128264.explanation: Yes, if homes remain empty for a long time, this should be made mandatory. People are withholding homes from home seekers.

128265.explanation: No truck traffic through built-up areas is no longer realistic. Pollution and nuisance affect the living environment of residents.

128266.explanation: Introducing paid parking at Walburg is fine; we would like the first hour to be free. So residents can do their shopping and to ensure that no entrepreneurs have to close because of this measure.

128208.explanation: The residents of Zwijndrecht hold a 'referendum' every four years, called an election. Especially for important issues, council members, as representatives of their constituents, are the ones who can make a well-considered decision.

128209.explanation: If residents see opportunities for this, the municipality should be able to cooperate under certain conditions.

128211.explanation: There should be plenty of room for the hospitality industry to determine their own opening hours. However, complete freedom is not desirable because there can be reasons (for example, nuisance) to limit opening times.

128212.explanation: The municipality can and may support and help such initiatives to get off the ground (possibly with a start-up subsidy). However, financial support is a form of income policy that the municipality should not engage in.

128214.explanation: There are very clear agreements about the current asylum seekers center (azc): closure after three years. The azc opened at the end of April 2024, so it will close at the end of April 2027. Immediate closure is only considered in case of severe nuisance in the area.

128215.explanation: 'The government' already determines on many fronts what is 'good' for residents. Setting conditions on use (with a heating guide) is fine, but a total ban is excessive.

128218.explanation: For certain groups of less affluent residents, that is still conceivable, but making it 'free' only means a shift to the general tax. Then even those who bring their bulky waste themselves also contribute.

128219.explanation: We should give more space to promote such activities. There are already clear standards; let's not make it unnecessarily difficult for organizations that arrange such parties.

128221.explanation: A local (or regional) broadcaster is important for information provision in a municipality. To avoid being (too) dependent on national broadcasters, we must support the local broadcaster.

128223.explanation: Music lessons are very important but truly belong to the basic tasks of education. The funding for this should therefore come from the national government.

128226.explanation: We see that extra enforcement efforts have an effect on livability. The examples from the past year show this is necessary. Together, we want a safe Zwijndrecht.

128227.explanation: Camera surveillance can help as an additional tool to keep the municipality safe. It must be used proportionately. We do not want a municipality that monitors everyone.

128229.explanation: The VVD has traditionally been a party that attaches great importance to order. Looking at the behavior towards our emergency services (ambulance, fire brigade, and police) nationwide, stricter action by our enforcement officers should generally be possible, also in Zwijndrecht.

128235.explanation: If this helps solve the housing shortage for this specific target group, the municipality can certainly contribute.

128236.explanation: Anyone who is able to should be asked for a contribution in return for welfare benefits. After all, it concerns public money. It also helps reduce the distance to the labor market and provides people with meaningful daily activities.

128238.explanation: We see that a significant group relies on allowances. Generous schemes create a group stuck in the known income trap because working (more) is not rewarding. This should never be the starting point for decisions about whether to work or not.

128241.explanation: Where possible, certainly yes, as it promotes traffic safety. But it simply cannot be everywhere. Especially not on main roads. We must also continue to consider our emergency services.

128243.explanation: A nonsense idea. Promoting cleaner transportation is fine. And with current national government policy, we are naturally moving in that direction. But a municipality, where a highway runs right through it, banning vehicles from neighborhoods?

128244.explanation: Where possible, choice should be possible, but if this means costs rise, there must also be a possibility for the government to (somewhat) limit freedom of choice.

128246.explanation: Of course, it is a good goal to combat period poverty. But as a municipality, we are entering a slippery slope about what we are and are not 'responsible' for.

128247.explanation: Of course, as VVD, we are for low (lower) taxes, but we must be honest. Taxes almost never go down. Everything in life becomes more expensive, and the municipality also faces ever-increasing costs. So let's be frugal as a municipality but don't sell myths.

128249.explanation: If necessary, this should be possible. Increasing employment within the municipality is good for residents (jobs, shorter travel distances). However, the first gains can be found by using existing business parks more effectively.

128250.explanation: Where possible, entrepreneurs should determine their own opening hours. Our retail sector already has a hard time due to all the online options. Online has no restrictions on opening hours either. However, let's continue to respect each other and maintain Sunday rest.

128251.explanation: Of course, status holders currently face a disadvantage in the housing market, but the pressure on social rental homes is so great that this group displaces 'local residents'. The solution ultimately lies in limiting inflow into the Netherlands.

128255.explanation: The VVD is not against school swimming. But in this case, money is not the problem. For residents with low incomes, there is Stichting Leergeld. What we hear from schools is that they cannot (want to) fit it into their schedule.

128257.explanation: Zwijndrecht has been striving for a more balanced population composition for years. 37% of our homes are (cheap) social rental homes. More than 80% of all homes in Zwijndrecht are affordable. Let our neighboring municipality, with less than 25% social rental housing, build more.

128263.explanation: We have a (very) skewed housing stock in Zwijndrecht. We therefore assume building in the (mid)expensive segment. This will automatically make cheaper housing available again. We must bring more balance to the housing stock.

128264.explanation: We would like to agree with this because no party in Zwijndrecht wants unnecessary vacancy. But there is no legal basis to make this mandatory. We can require owners to consult with us, but not force them to rent out.

128265.explanation: Of course, the VVD wants to prevent as much as possible that truck traffic passes through the center of Zwijndrecht. But closing this simply shifts the problem and even causes more residents to suffer from 'nuisance' overall.

128266.explanation: Besides the fact that the VVD is against paid parking, we decided to abolish it in 2016 for a reason. We see the positive effect for retailers and see that nuisance in the surrounding streets has strongly decreased.

128208.explanation: A referendum is not the right tool. However, we do support involving residents in the lead-up to a topic, for example through consultation evenings.

128209.explanation: Not a sports hall, but certainly a park or other public places. We encourage residents to challenge the municipality, for instance regarding the design of public spaces.

128211.explanation: Different rules apply to these kinds of matters. However, we want to support hospitality entrepreneurs when they encounter challenges.

128212.explanation: The CDA wants the municipality to actively support sustainability initiatives from the community. This includes initiatives like greening neighborhoods, replacing tiles with greenery, and researching neighborhood batteries.

128214.explanation: We have an agreement with the COA until April 2027 for the current asylum seekers' center. The CDA is not in favor of closing it earlier.

128215.explanation: This is not an issue according to the CDA.

128218.explanation: The CDA supports a low fee for bulky waste collection to prevent people from simply dumping bulky waste on the street. However, we believe the first collection should be free.

128219.explanation: The noise standards for festivals in Zwijndrecht are already properly coordinated with the organizers. This coordination takes place between organizers, the municipality, and enforcement.

128221.explanation: This is not an issue in the municipality of Zwijndrecht. Subsidies are already provided and are more than sufficient to enable the local broadcaster to operate.

128223.explanation: According to the CDA, this is a decision that schools should make themselves. Schools control their own curricula. If they want this, resources are already available.

128226.explanation: We support more safety on the streets. If it turns out that more enforcement officers and public officials (boa's) are needed, then we are in favor.

128227.explanation: The CDA supports this, provided the situation warrants it. At our initiative, a mobile camera has already been installed; if necessary, we are in favor of more.

128229.explanation: The CDA supports enhancing the sense of safety. If a request comes to expand the equipment of our boa's, we will agree to it.

128235.explanation: The CDA wants to choose the sustainable option. Better traffic flow so that starters can have a fair chance. Temporary housing is not a solution to the problem.

128236.explanation: Agreed, but the CDA does not want to make this mandatory. We should facilitate those who are able.

128238.explanation: The income limit is already more generous than the national average. We want to focus more on other ways to help people in need.

128241.explanation: As far as the CDA is concerned, the major access roads should remain at 50 km/h.

128243.explanation: Given the type of municipality Zwijndrecht is, this is not yet a logical choice.

128244.explanation: The CDA believes this should be possible when it can be, but if there are no alternatives, this should not be a reason to refuse care.

128246.explanation: The municipality is not involved in this. Initiatives already exist in society to make this possible.

128247.explanation: An increase, but also a decrease, is, according to the CDA, not simply applicable.

128249.explanation: Zwijndrecht has limited space. Instead of business parks, the CDA prefers development with housing and green spaces where appropriate.

128250.explanation: We already have policy on this, supported by the CDA.

128251.explanation: Several groups are entitled to a priority scheme, including status holders. Also, divorced parents or people coming from other difficult situations have priority in the form of an urgency declaration.

128255.explanation: The municipality does not have authority over this.

128257.explanation: Zwijndrecht already more than meets the quota for social rental housing. We choose not to demolish social housing but rather to build more in the middle and higher price segments.

128263.explanation: We continue to focus on building homes in the middle and higher price segments. This way, we improve the housing market's flow and make affordable homes available for our starters.

128264.explanation: No obligation. But the CDA does believe better policy is needed regarding vacancy.

128265.explanation: Closing the Blauwe Brug does not make the municipality safer. It merely shifts the problem to other places where traffic safety is actually worse.

128266.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of reintroducing paid parking, but it is not off the table. For example, if municipal taxes had to be raised instead of reintroducing paid parking.

128208.explanation: D66 wants to give people more influence over decisions that affect them. A Citizens’ Assembly would be a good example of this in Zwijndrecht.

128209.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to support associations and volunteers, including when they take on management tasks. This way, the municipality remains a place we all feel jointly responsible for. Residents of Zwijndrecht also have the right to take over municipal tasks.

128211.explanation: Nationally, partly due to D66, pilots have been conducted where opening hours are extended in consultation with entrepreneurs and residents. Important in this is community support and limiting potential nuisance.

128212.explanation: Sustainability of homes remains a priority for D66 Zwijndrecht. Energy-efficient homes are good for the climate and reduce living costs in the long term. The municipality assists residents and housing associations with advice and support (for example, with permit applications) for sustainable investments.

128214.explanation: D66 Zwijndrecht believes we should provide a safe home for refugees and that every municipality should contribute to this.

128215.explanation: D66 encourages 'clean stoves' - residents who use fireplaces that meet the latest standards. It is also very important to raise awareness of tools like the 'Stookwijzer' so residents know on which days it is better not to burn fuel.

128218.explanation: A clean street starts with all of us. If everyone contributes, our municipality remains a pleasant place to live. The municipality ensures reliable waste collection and tackles litter. D66 Zwijndrecht wants residents to be able to dispose of bulky waste for free once a year.

128219.explanation: The current standards for noise nuisance are sufficient.

128221.explanation: D66 supports a strong local broadcaster but believes it, like other organizations, should first use the existing subsidy scheme. Extra money is not needed now.

128223.explanation: Culture makes a municipality vibrant. D66 Zwijndrecht wants to continue investing in our library, music school, music in schools, local venues, and cultural associations.

128226.explanation: We do not think more boa's need to be added. We do find their role important. We want them to be integrated and to signal what is happening in society.

128227.explanation: Cameras should be placed at strategic locations where there is a need. Preventive placement of cameras. As far as we are concerned, only temporarily and in places causing nuisance.

128229.explanation: Weapons are never the solution in principle. Weapons initially provoke more violence. We stand for a stronger police force, but they retain the monopoly on violence. They are well trained for this.

128235.explanation: First and foremost, we must work on adequate housing in general. Due to the absence of a college or university in Zwijndrecht, student housing does not get high priority.

128236.explanation: D66 Zwijndrecht considers it very important that people receiving social assistance get support towards employment where possible.

128238.explanation: Thanks in part to D66, Zwijndrecht has a generous poverty policy, which theoretically enables residents in poverty, when they utilize all subsidy options, to just get by.

128241.explanation: Speed of car traffic is an important cause of feeling unsafe. Where possible, and considering emergency services, D66 Zwijndrecht believes the maximum speed within built-up areas should be 30 km/h.

128243.explanation: Introducing an environmental zone does not solve anything in a municipality the size of Zwijndrecht. Neighborhoods are close together and the Walburg shopping center still needs to be supplied. D66 Zwijndrecht advocates other ways to achieve cleaner air and less nuisance.

128244.explanation: For D66, everyone is equal and everyone should be free to make their own choices.

128246.explanation: D66 Zwijndrecht has always campaigned against period poverty. We consider it very important that residents who cannot afford it themselves are supported, for example through the Food Bank or schools.

128247.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is an important source of income for the municipality that finances facilities for all residents.

128249.explanation: Space is scarce and must be used carefully. When there is specific demand for expanding business parks and that demand cannot be met otherwise, D66 Zwijndrecht is willing to consider the possibilities for expansion.

128250.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should allow entrepreneurs the freedom to open on Sundays. Whether entrepreneurs or visitors make use of this is up to them.

128251.explanation: Refugees genuinely have absolutely nothing. If it involves a status holder, the municipality must fulfill the nationally agreed responsibility. By the way, this concerns a small percentage of social rental homes.

128255.explanation: Discussions with schools in the past have shown they do not want this. There is already so much pressure on teaching time. Children can apply to Leergeld Foundation when there are insufficient financial resources for swimming lessons.

128257.explanation: In Zwijndrecht, about 35% of the homes are social rental housing. Therefore, D66 Zwijndrecht focuses on building in the middle and high market segments, where there is also a significant shortage.

128263.explanation: In Zwijndrecht, about 35% of the homes are social rental housing, a number well above the national average in the Netherlands. Therefore, D66 Zwijndrecht focuses on building for the middle and high segments, where there is also a significant shortage.

128264.explanation: We understand this line of thought but do not believe an owner can be obligated to do so.

128265.explanation: The route over the Blauwe Brug was designed with freight traffic in mind: wide roundabouts, separated lanes by a central reservation, spacious and clearly arranged.

128266.explanation: For now, reintroducing paid parking at the Walburg shopping center is not necessary, but if the traffic situation requires it, we will agree to it.

128208.explanation: Especially when it concerns the immediate living environment and safety.

128209.explanation: However, keep in mind that the profits should also be reinvested in events for the neighborhood.

128212.explanation: Such citizen initiatives should always be supported.

128214.explanation: Provided clear rules are applied and there is no nuisance or crimes. In case of crimes, strict enforcement is required. The safety of residents must never be jeopardized. The safety of our own citizens comes first.

128215.explanation: According to the environmental guidelines that must be adhered to.

128218.explanation: Fixed days per month to combat littering and promote recycling.

128219.explanation: We cannot assess that. We have never heard that it caused any nuisance.

128221.explanation: Absolutely, because this is in everyone's interest. This keeps people well informed about what is happening in Zwijndrecht.

128223.explanation: Equal rights, equal opportunities. Making music fosters togetherness; no distinction should be made. Introduce music lessons in primary school.

128226.explanation: At locations where this is necessary.

128227.explanation: Certainly in places where this is desired.

128229.explanation: Only if they are allowed to use it when they themselves are attacked.

128235.explanation: Provided they are actually enrolled in a study program.

128236.explanation: Provided physical and mental health permits this.

128238.explanation: There is a suspicion that there is a lot of hidden poverty among the elderly and single households.

128241.explanation: Where necessary, of course, but where 50 km/h speed is possible, it is unnecessary.

128244.explanation: No one may ever be excluded based on religion, origin or belief.

128247.explanation: The current costs are already high enough for everyone.

128249.explanation: Provided that safety, environment and health of residents are not compromised.

128263.explanation: It would be nice if this were an equivalent percentage.

128264.explanation: Certainly, because there is a severe housing shortage.

128265.explanation: Freight traffic can cause dangerous situations for residents. The highway is a safe alternative.

128266.explanation: This could come at the expense of the turnover of the shopkeepers.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce paid parking at the Walburg shopping center.
Business parks in Zwijndrecht should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Zwijndrecht should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The residents of Zwijndrecht should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zwijndrecht moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Social housing should be built in the future Noordoevers neighborhood.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: In Zwijndrecht the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Zwijndrecht moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Zwijndrecht should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The Blue Bridge should remain open to trucks.
The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
At least 35% of the new homes to be built in Zwijndrecht should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Zwijndrecht moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who carry out safety checks in Zwijndrecht should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Zwijndrecht controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Zwijndrecht should introduce a low-emission zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow fewer Sunday shopping days.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should require owners of vacant houses to rent them to people seeking housing.
